bp_title: Implement least privilege access permissions for foundation models accessing data stores
question_stem: GENSEC01 — How do you manage access to generative AI endpoints? (BP03 re-verified 2026-06-10 from gensec01-bp03.html for the step-3 walk.)
verbatim_text: GENSEC01-BP03 step 3 — Develop a data ingestion pipeline which obfuscates or removes data that should not be processed by a foundation model. Examples of this data might be personal information. The scope of this data is informed largely by the workload use case. Ingest this data from your data lake into the vector store lake house. Sub-bullets: (a) A use case for a customer service assistant may require access to handbooks, documentation, and customer service material, not company financials, staff information or HR policies. (b) Sanitizing for prohibited material should happen before the model accesses the data, at time of ingestion.
outcome_note: PROMOTED 2026-06-10 → GS1B3-02 (Never ingest what the model should never process), 21st standard, sixth Security principle, focus area P21. Resolves the PII-removal deferral parked at the 2026-06-07 GS1B3-01 walk; first one-BP-two-standards case (GS1B3-01 step 4 = read gate / who retrieves; GS1B3-02 step 3 = write gate / what enters the store). HALT waived — user-directed promote-and-complete after the deferral was discussed twice in-session. step_promotion 3/3/3/3. Contract: sanitisation manifest + stage wired ahead of the store on every ingestion path (routed-ingestion AST lint) + per-run audit records + quarterly seeded-PII audit. Enforcement limit appended to enforcement_limits.md. not_prompted.json deferred entry removed. No open security-pillar residue remains.
open_items: JSON-parse verify data/principles.json and data/not_prompted.json from terminal. S3 re-upload for the live app. AIGP IV.C review (GS1B3-02 flagged as strongest candidate for a privacy/data-governance competency).
next_candidates: GENOPS02 (3 BPs); GENOPS05; GENCOST03-BP02; GENREL pillar; GENPERF04-BP01 formal sweep.
